title: Team-EV-Turkey
text:
This is our project webpage.
Please stay tuned to follow our project activities.
Welcome to the project webpage of Team-EV-Turkey! This website hosts our reproducible workflow and interactive findings for the EMU430 Data Analytics course.

title: Team-EV-Turkey
text:
The Core Question
Regional Bottleneck Identification: By mapping EV numbers against the number of charging sockets per province, where are the most significant supply/demand discrepancies and infrastructure gaps located?

title: Team-EV-Turkey
text:
Presentation Roadmap

The Context: Why EVs and why now?
The Engine: How we cleaned the EPDK provincial data (Fuzzy Matching).
The Forecast: Projecting the 2027 EV landscape (Log-Linear Models).
The Solution: Identifying “Investment Hot Spots” and infrastructure gaps.

title: Insights & 2027 Projections
text:
Important🚀 Key Executive Takeaways




Exponential Growth: Adoption of EVs in Turkey is proceeding exponentially, not linearly. The market is accelerating faster than traditional forecasts.
Investment Bottlenecks: Based on our 2027 estimates, there will be a critical shortage of charging stations not only in urban centers but particularly in transit-route provinces.
Actionable Prioritization: At a benchmark of “50 EVs per station,” immediate capital injection is required in the critical (red) zones mapped below to sustain Turkey’s EV targets.

title: Insights & 2027 Projections
Insights & 2027 Projections
text:
2. 📈 Market Growth Trend (Top 5 Hubs)
When examining the time-series growth chart of the top 5 provinces, the exponential curve is undeniable.


Click to View Trend Analysis Code
# ---- Latest snapshot per province -------------------------------------------
latest_snapshot <- analysis_ready |>
  group_by(sehir, province_key) |>
  filter(date == max(date)) |>
  summarise(
    current_stations = istasyon_sayisi,
    current_evs      = ev_sayisi,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ---- Shared theme -----------------------------------------------------------
theme_ev <- function() {
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 15),
      plot.subtitle    = element_text(color = "grey40", size = 11),
      plot.caption     = element_text(color = "grey50", size = 9),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      legend.position  = "right"
    )
}

# ---- PLOT 1: EV growth trend in major cities --------------------------------
top_5_cities <- latest_snapshot |>
  slice_max(current_evs, n = 5) |>
  pull(sehir)

p1 <- ggplot(analysis_ready |> filter(sehir %in% top_5_cities),
             aes(x = date, y = ev_sayisi, color = sehir)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.1) +
  geom_point(size = 2.4) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_comma()) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Province") +
  labs(
    title    = "EV Adoption Trend in Top-5 Provinces",
    subtitle = "Monthly registered electric vehicles",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Registered EVs",
    caption  = "Source: EPDK"
  ) +
  theme_ev()

print(p1)

title: Insights & 2027 Projections
text:
3. 🎯 2027 Projections & Investment Gaps
We forecasted the 2027 EV count using a Log-Linear Model and identified provincial investment gaps based on an optimal benchmark of 50 EVs per station.







Province Tier
Action Required by 2027




🔴 Critical Risk
Immediate capital injection; infrastructure is severely lagging.


🟠 High Risk
Infrastructure expansion must double to meet forecasted demand.


🟢 Adequate
Current growth pace is sufficient.





Click to View 2027 Forecast & Gap Analysis Code
# ---- Forecast EVs to 2027 ---------------------------------------------------
target_date_num <- as.numeric(as.Date("2027-01-01"))

forecast_2027 <- analysis_ready |>
  group_by(sehir, province_key) |>
  group_modify(~{
    df <- .x
    if (nrow(df) < 3 || length(unique(df$ev_sayisi)) < 2) {
      return(tibble(ev_2027_linear = NA_real_,
                    ev_2027_loglin = NA_real_,
                    cagr_pct       = NA_real_))
    }
    lin <- lm(ev_sayisi ~ as.numeric(date), data = df)
    pred_lin <- as.numeric(predict(lin, newdata = data.frame(date = target_date_num)))

    if (any(df$ev_sayisi <= 0)) {
      pred_log <- NA_real_; cagr <- NA_real_
    } else {
      llm <- lm(log(ev_sayisi) ~ as.numeric(date), data = df)
      slope <- coef(llm)[2]
      pred_log <- exp(as.numeric(predict(llm, newdata = data.frame(date = target_date_num))))
      cagr <- (exp(slope * 365) - 1) * 100
    }
    tibble(ev_2027_linear = round(pmax(pred_lin, 0)),
           ev_2027_loglin = round(pmax(pred_log, 0)),
           cagr_pct       = round(cagr, 1))
  }) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(ev_2027 = coalesce(ev_2027_loglin, ev_2027_linear))

# ---- Priority scoring -------------------------------------------------------
TARGET_EV_PER_STATION <- 50

priority_table <- forecast_2027 |>
  inner_join(latest_snapshot, by = c("sehir", "province_key")) |>
  mutate(
    stations_needed_2027  = ceiling(ev_2027 / TARGET_EV_PER_STATION),
    investment_gap        = pmax(stations_needed_2027 - current_stations, 0),
    bottleneck_ratio_2027 = ev_2027 / pmax(current_stations, 1),
    priority_tier = case_when(
      ev_2027 > 15000 & bottleneck_ratio_2027 > 100 ~ "Critical",
      ev_2027 > 5000  & bottleneck_ratio_2027 > 75  ~ "High",
      bottleneck_ratio_2027 > 50                    ~ "Medium",
      TRUE                                          ~ "Adequate"
    ),
    priority_tier = factor(priority_tier,
                           levels = c("Critical", "High", "Medium", "Adequate"))
  ) |>
  arrange(desc(investment_gap))

# ---- PLOT 2: Supply-vs-demand gap (dumbbell chart) --------------------------
gap_plot_data <- priority_table |>
  slice_max(investment_gap, n = 15) |>
  mutate(sehir = fct_reorder(sehir, investment_gap))

p2 <- ggplot(gap_plot_data) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = current_stations, xend = stations_needed_2027,
                   y = sehir, yend = sehir),
               color = "grey70", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(aes(x = current_stations,     y = sehir,
                 color = "Current stations"), size = 4) +
  geom_point(aes(x = stations_needed_2027, y = sehir,
                 color = "Stations needed by 2027"), size = 4) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Current stations"        = "#1f77b4",
               "Stations needed by 2027" = "#d62728"),
    name = NULL) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = label_comma()) +
  labs(
    title    = "Charging Infrastructure Gap by 2027",
    subtitle = paste0("Top-15 provinces by investment gap  ·  benchmark = ",
                      TARGET_EV_PER_STATION, " EVs per station"),
    x        = "Number of charging stations",
    y        = NULL,
    caption  = "Forecast: log-linear model on monthly EPDK data"
  ) +
  theme_ev() +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

print(p2)

title: Insights & 2027 Projections
text:
4. 🗺️ Turkey 2027: The Interactive Heatmap
This is where the investments must go. The static map shows the risk distribution, while the Interactive Map below calculates the exact USD required to close the gap in each province (assuming a $50k cost per DC fast charger).






Note



Hover Instructions: Use your mouse to explore the interactive map below to view specific 2027 EV forecasts, missing stations, and required capital per province.




Click to View Spatial & Interactive Mapping Code
# ---- Build spatial layer ----------------------------------------------------
tr_map <- ne_states(country = "turkey", returnclass = "sf") |>
  mutate(
    province_raw = name,
    province_key = normalize_name(name)
  )

manual_fixes <- tribble(
  ~ne_key,        ~tuik_key,
  "afyon",        "afyonkarahisar",
  "kmaras",       "kahramanmaras",
  "kahramanmars", "kahramanmaras",
  "icel",         "mersin",
  "urfa",         "sanliurfa",
  "zinguldak",    "zonguldak"
)

tr_map <- tr_map |>
  left_join(manual_fixes, by = c("province_key" = "ne_key")) |>
  mutate(province_key = coalesce(tuik_key, province_key)) |>
  select(-tuik_key)

map_plot_data <- tr_map |>
  left_join(priority_table, by = "province_key")

# ---- PLOT 3: Static heat map ------------------------------------------------
map_plot_data_cap <- map_plot_data |>
  mutate(bottleneck_capped = pmin(bottleneck_ratio_2027, 200))

p3 <- ggplot(map_plot_data_cap) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = bottleneck_capped), color = "white", linewidth = 0.15) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors   = c("#fff5eb", "#fdae61", "#d7191c"),
    values   = c(0, 0.5, 1),
    na.value = "grey85",
    name     = "EVs per station\n(2027 forecast)",
    breaks   = c(50, 100, 150, 200),
    labels   = c("50", "100", "150", "200+")
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Turkey 2027 Charging Infrastructure Bottleneck Map",
    subtitle = "Red provinces require urgent charging-station investment"
  ) +
  theme_void(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

print(p3)









Click to View Spatial & Interactive Mapping Code
# ---- Interactive Leaflet map ------------------------------------------------
AVG_STATION_COST_USD <- 50000

map_plot_data_interactive <- map_plot_data |>
  mutate(
    investment_cost_usd = investment_gap * AVG_STATION_COST_USD,
    cost_label = scales::dollar(investment_cost_usd, prefix = "$"),
    ev_label   = scales::comma(ev_2027, accuracy = 1)
  )

pal <- colorBin(
  palette  = "YlOrRd",
  domain   = map_plot_data_interactive$investment_gap,
  bins     = c(0, 10, 50, 100, 250, 500, Inf)
)

interactive_map <- leaflet(data = map_plot_data_interactive) |>
  addProviderTiles(providers$CartoDB.Positron) |>
  addPolygons(
    fillColor   = ~pal(investment_gap),
    weight      = 1,
    opacity     = 1,
    color       = "white",
    dashArray   = "3",
    fillOpacity = 0.8,
    highlightOptions = highlightOptions(
      weight = 3, color = "#666", dashArray = "", fillOpacity = 0.9, bringToFront = TRUE
    ),
    label = ~paste0(
      province_raw,
      " | 2027 EV Forecast: ", ev_label,
      " | Missing Stations: ", investment_gap,
      " | Required Investment: ", cost_label
    ) |> lapply(htmltools::HTML)
  ) |>
  addLegend(
    pal      = pal,
    values   = ~investment_gap,
    opacity  = 0.7,
    title    = "2027 Missing Stations<br>(Investment Opportunity)",
    position = "bottomright"
  )

print(interactive_map)

title: Data Processing & Methodology
text:
To ensure transparency and reliability, the foundational dataset for our project was compiled from a unified official source:

EPDK (Energy Market Regulatory Authority): Provincial-level data containing the number of registered electric vehicles and the geographical distribution of charging stations (sockets).

title: Data Processing & Methodology
text:
To ensure transparency and reliability, the foundational dataset for our project was compiled from a unified official source:

EPDK (Energy Market Regulatory Authority): Provincial-level data containing the number of registered electric vehicles and the geographical distribution of charging stations (sockets).

title: Data Processing & Methodology
text:
🛠️ Preprocessing & “Fuzzy Matching”
The most significant challenge we faced while merging raw datasets was the formatting of city names (e.g., encoding errors, typos, HTML tags).
To solve this without introducing human error, we built a “City Repair” Engine:

The Problem: Corrupted data entries like “Afyonkara” or “Istanbul ”.
The Solution: Fuzzy Matching Algorithm.
The Tool: The stringdist package in R.







TipTechnical Insight



Every provincial entry was automatically cross-referenced and corrected against an official benchmark list of 81 provinces using a maximum distance metric (amatch). This ensured 100% data integrity before modeling.


The following is our reproducible code pipeline for cleaning the datasets:


Click to View Data Cleaning & City Matching Code
# 1. Libraries
if (!require("tidyverse")) install.packages("tidyverse")
if (!require("stringdist")) install.packages("stringdist") # For smart/fuzzy matching
library(tidyverse)
library(stringdist)

# 2. Directories (Using relative paths for reproducibility)
data_path <- "data/" 
output_path <- "clear_data/"
if (!dir.exists(output_path)) dir.create(output_path)

file_list <- list.files(path = data_path, pattern = "^EV_Data_.*\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)

# 3. OFFICIAL TURKEY PROVINCE LIST (Benchmark)
official_cities <- c(
  "Adana", "Adıyaman", "Afyonkarahisar", "Ağrı", "Amasya", "Ankara", "Antalya", "Artvin",
  "Aydın", "Balıkesir", "Bilecik", "Bingöl", "Bitlis", "Bolu", "Burdur", "Bursa", "Çanakkale",
  "Çankırı", "Çorum", "Denizli", "Diyarbakır", "Edirne", "Elazığ", "Erzincan", "Erzurum",
  "Eskişehir", "Gaziantep", "Giresun", "Gümüşhane", "Hakkari", "Hatay", "Isparta", "Mersin",
  "İstanbul", "İzmir", "Kars", "Kastamonu", "Kayseri", "Kırklareli", "Kırşehir", "Kocaeli",
  "Konya", "Kütahya", "Malatya", "Manisa", "Kahramanmaraş", "Mardin", "Muğla", "Muş",
  "Nevşehir", "Niğde", "Ordu", "Rize", "Sakarya", "Samsun", "Siirt", "Sinop", "Sivas",
  "Tekirdağ", "Tokat", "Trabzon", "Tunceli", "Şanlıurfa", "Uşak", "Van", "Yozgat", "Zonguldak",
  "Aksaray", "Bayburt", "Karaman", "Kırıkkale", "Batman", "Şırnak", "Bartın", "Ardahan",
  "Iğdır", "Yalova", "Karabük", "Kilis", "Osmaniye", "Düzce"
)

# 4. CITY REPAIR ENGINE (Fuzzy Matching)
clean_and_match_city <- function(messy_city) {
  # Clean up garbage characters first
  cleaned <- messy_city %>%
    str_replace_all("&[[:alpha:]]+;", "") %>% # Remove HTML entities
    str_replace_all("[^[:alnum:] ]", " ") %>% # Remove non-alphanumeric characters
    str_replace_all("Ã", "Ç") %>%             # Pre-cleaning for common encoding errors
    str_to_lower(locale = "tr") %>%
    str_squish()
  
  # If the city name is highly corrupted, find the closest official name
  match_idx <- amatch(cleaned, str_to_lower(official_cities, locale = "tr"), maxDist = 5)
  
  if (!is.na(match_idx)) {
    return(official_cities[match_idx])
  } else {
    return(str_to_title(cleaned, locale = "tr"))
  }
}

# 5. File Processing
process_file <- function(f) {
  tarih_val <- str_extract(basename(f), "\\d{4}_\\d{2}")
  
  # Encoding-independent read attempt
  d <- read.csv(f, stringsAsFactors = FALSE, check.names = FALSE, fileEncoding = "UTF-8")
  if (ncol(d) < 5) d <- read.csv(f, stringsAsFactors = FALSE, check.names = FALSE, fileEncoding = "CP1254")
  
  # Force assign column names
  if (ncol(d) == 5) {
    names(d) <- c("sehir", "istasyon", "otomobil", "ev", "oran")
  } else {
    names(d) <- c("sehir", "istasyon", "degisim", "otomobil", "ev", "oran")
  }
  
  d %>%
    as_tibble() %>%
    mutate(
      tarih = tarih_val,
      # Compare and fix each city against the official list
      sehir = map_chr(sehir, clean_and_match_city),
      otomobil_sayisi = as.numeric(str_remove_all(otomobil, "[^0-9]")),
      ev_sayisi = as.numeric(str_remove_all(ev, "[^0-9]")),
      istasyon_sayisi = as.numeric(str_remove_all(istasyon, "[^0-9]")),
      istasyon_doluluk_orani = as.numeric(str_replace_all(oran, ",", "."))
    ) %>%
    select(tarih, sehir, otomobil_sayisi, ev_sayisi, istasyon_sayisi, istasyon_doluluk_orani)
}

# 6. Merge and Save
final_df <- file_list %>%
  map_dfr(process_file) %>%
  arrange(sehir, tarih) %>%
  distinct(tarih, sehir, .keep_all = TRUE)

title: Data Processing & Methodology
text:
📦 Final Dataset
Our fully processed, cleaned, and merged dataset is packed in .RData format for analysis and modeling.
Download Cleaned Dataset (.RData)
